policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two clean local Node 22/V8 coverage runs on 2026-07-17 passed 1244 tests, and the final clean run passed 1249 tests; all three measured the identical complete 2253-line core scope at 1932 covered lines (85.752%). Issue #122's localized/late home-row regressions exercise existing navigation and action-sheet paths, advancing 17 covered lines over the reviewed 1915/2253 baseline without changing scope. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Theme Studio curated catalog and compatibility work in issue #387. The clean GitHub Actions .NET 10.0.10/Coverlet run on 2026-07-19 passed all 1820 tests over the complete 29150-line assembly scope and measured 20707 covered lines. The reviewed high-water is 20707/29150 (71.036%). Relative to issue #384's 20669/29111 baseline, the legitimate 39-line scope increase adds the curated catalog provenance, schema allowlists, Jellyfish import migrations, administrator palette defaults, and their server tests; 38 of those net-new lines are covered, and total assembly coverage improves from 71.001% to 71.036%. The existing four-line low-side tolerance is unchanged, spans the already reproduced Coverlet concurrency variance, and remains far below the policy maximum; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
